heading: Objective
body: Teach salespeople and managers how to introduce manager involvement early, position the manager as guest support, align around what matters most, and protect unsold opportunities through Save-A-Deal follow-up.

heading: Guest-Support Move
body: The manager introduction is not a closing move. It is a guest-support move.
bullets: Normalize early|Support the guest|Protect the process

heading: Timing Changes Meaning
body: A manager introduced early feels like support. A manager introduced only after concern can feel like escalation.

heading: Introduce Early
body: The manager should be introduced after early discovery and before the visit reaches numbers or exit risk.

heading: Primary Word Track
body: At some point early, I’ll introduce you to one of our managers. We do that so we can make sure we are building the right plan around what matters most to you.

heading: Manager Flow
body: Managers must know who is in the showroom, what stage each guest is in, and where support is needed.
bullets: Who is here|Stage reached|Manager introduced|Next action

heading: Unsold Guest Review
body: Every unsold opportunity needs a real reason, next best action, owner, and follow-up time.

heading: Planner
body: Use the worksheet to document manager introduction, guest support alignment, and Save-A-Deal follow-up.
